title: energizing and buttery vietnamese morbier pho
description: a vietnamese-inspired dish featuring morbier cheese, whisked eggs, and flavorful spices, perfect for a snack or light meal
instructions: heat 1 tablespoon of butter in a large skillet over medium heat.
cook the rice noodles according to package instructions and set aside.
in a blender or food processor, combine the whisked eggs, fish sauce, and red chili flakes and blend until smooth.
heat another tablespoon of butter in the skillet and add the blended egg mixture, cook until set, breaking up with a spatula as needed.
add the sliced cabbage, ginger, and garlic to the skillet and cook until the cabbage is tender, about 5 minutes.
add the cooked noodles, tofu, coconut milk, and green onions to the skillet and stir to combine.
season with salt and black pepper to taste.
transfer the noodle mixture to a serving dish and top with sliced morbier cheese.
bake in a 375°f oven for 5-7 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
serve hot, garnished with cilantro and lime wedges.
divide into 4-6 servings depending on serving size.
note: for a vegan version, omit the eggs and use a flax egg substitute.
